architectural_notes: Admin panel authentication switched from principal-based to user ID-based access control
Admin panel authentication will revert from password-based back to Internet Identity principal-based access control
known_issues: AI clip generation feature is not implemented despite being listed as completed in existing features
Admin panel stuck in 'checking permissions' state instead of displaying content
Admin panel user ID display feature not working - the user ID section is not rendering despite implementation
Admin panel displaying 'Checking permissions...' message but not progressing to show admin dashboard content after user verification
Admin panel navigation button not appearing in the header for authenticated users despite Internet Identity login

existing_features.title: Create a form where users can input a YouTube video URL
Display the YouTube video in an embedded player after URL submission
Implement clip selection controls allowing users to set start and end timestamps for a video segment
Display a list of all saved clips with thumbnail, title, and time range
Allow users to delete saved clips

Design a creative, coherent visual theme with distinct style for the video clipping application interface, ensuring consistent application of colors, typography, and layout patterns throughout all screens
Display AI-generated clip suggestions in the frontend with viral potential indicators
Add visual indicators or badges to saved clips showing their viral potential rating
Update the application header branding in the Layout component to display 'Beast Clipping' instead of 'ClipTube'
Update the HTML page title in index.html to 'Beast Clipping' instead of any reference to 'ClipTube'
Create a React hook useIsOwner that calls the backend isOwner method and returns the result using React Query
Create an AdminPanel component that displays system statistics including total clips count, total users count, and trending clips analytics
Add an Admin navigation link in the Layout header that only renders when the user is the owner
Create an /admin route in App.tsx that displays the AdminPanel component only if the user is the owner, otherwise shows an access denied message
Fix the JavaScript error in the admin panel that causes the entire application to crash with a blank screen and 'A problem repeatedly occurred' error
Add proper error boundary handling in the admin route to prevent admin-specific errors from crashing the entire application
Ensure useIsOwner hook handles loading and error states correctly without causing render crashes
Add fallback UI in AdminPanel component for cases where statistics fail to load
Create a React hook useAutoGenerateClips that calls the backend automatic clip generation method and handles the response using React Query
Update the ClipList component to indicate which clips were auto-generated versus manually created
Implement the useAutoGenerateClips hook in frontend/src/hooks/useAutoGenerateClips.ts to call the backend generateClipsAutomatically method and return the generated clips using React Query
Add a 'Generate AI Clips' button to the VideoUrlForm component that triggers automatic clip generation when a valid YouTube URL is entered
Update the App.tsx component to integrate automatic clip generation into the video workflow, storing generated suggestions and passing them to the ClipSuggestions component
Debug and fix the Layout component's admin navigation link rendering logic to ensure the 'Admin' button appears in the header when the authenticated user is the owner
Verify the useIsOwner hook is correctly returning ownership status and handling loading states without blocking the header from rendering

Modify the Layout component (frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) to ensure the Admin navigation button renders in the header for the authenticated owner, bypassing any conditional logic that may be preventing it from appearing. Add explicit console logging to track isOwner state and button render conditions.

Add defensive fallback rendering in Layout.tsx so that if useIsOwner hook returns undefined or takes too long to resolve, a temporary 'Check Admin' button still appears for authenticated users, allowing manual verification of admin access.

Review and fix the useIsOwner hook (frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts) to ensure it correctly returns the ownership status without blocking or returning false negatives. Add error logging and ensure React Query is configured with appropriate staleTime and refetchOnMount settings.

Add comprehensive console logging throughout the Layout component to trace the complete rendering lifecycle of the Admin navigation button, including: authentication state changes, useIsOwner hook return values, conditional rendering decisions, and any errors that may prevent the button from displaying

Verify that the useIsOwner hook in frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts is correctly calling the backend isCallerAdmin method and add detailed logging for the request, response, and any errors encountered during the call

Add explicit fallback rendering in the Layout component header that always displays a 'Debug: Check Admin Status' button for authenticated users, regardless of useIsOwner return value, to enable manual verification of the admin check functionality

Remove all conditional rendering logic for the Admin navigation button in the Layout component header and force it to always display when a user is authenticated, regardless of the useIsOwner hook return value

Add a permanent visual indicator (e.g., 'TEST MODE' badge or styling) to the Admin button to make it immediately obvious that admin access checking has been temporarily disabled for debugging purposes

Add browser console logging in the Layout component that outputs 'ADMIN BUTTON FORCED VISIBLE' when the component renders with an authenticated user, confirming the button rendering logic is executing
Read and analyze the Layout component (frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) to identify why the Admin navigation button is not rendering in the header despite multiple debugging attempts
Read and analyze the useIsOwner hook (frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts) to verify it correctly calls the backend isCallerAdmin method and returns the ownership status without false negatives
Based on the code analysis, implement a definitive fix for the Admin button visibility issue that ensures the button appears in the header for authenticated owners
Update the admin route protection in frontend/src/App.tsx to properly handle the isCallerAdmin check and display the AdminPanel component when the authenticated user is verified as owner
Verify the useIsOwner hook in frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts correctly processes the backend isCallerAdmin response and returns the admin status without false negatives
Add frontend console logging in the useIsOwner hook to display the authenticated user's principal and the backend's isCallerAdmin response, helping identify any principal mismatch issues
Add frontend console logging in the App.tsx admin route to display the isCallerAdmin check result and the access decision, confirming whether the access denial occurs in the frontend or backend
Read and analyze the useActor hook in frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts to verify the admin token from URL parameters is correctly extracted and passed to the backend during actor initialization
Verify the admin route protection logic in frontend/src/App.tsx correctly processes the isCallerAdmin response and grants access to the AdminPanel component when the authenticated user is the owner
Update the frontend App.tsx admin route protection to display the exact principal received from the backend isCallerAdmin check in the access denied message for debugging purposes

Add frontend console logging in the App.tsx admin route that outputs: 1) the exact isAdmin boolean value returned from the backend call, 2) the type of the isAdmin value (typeof isAdmin), 3) the result of explicit boolean checks (isAdmin === true, isAdmin === false), 4) any error objects if the backend call fails

Update the frontend App.tsx admin route to display more detailed debug information when access is denied, including the raw backend response, any error messages, and the exact boolean comparison result

Update the frontend App.tsx admin route to display clearer error messaging when access is denied, including the backend's isCallerAdmin response value, the authenticated principal, and instructions for resolving the access issue
Add a new section to the AdminPanel component that displays the currently authenticated user's principal ID prominently at the top of the dashboard for identity verification
Update the AdminPanel component to retrieve the authenticated user's principal from the useInternetIdentity hook and display it in the user ID section

Debug and fix the admin route component in frontend/src/App.tsx to identify why the permissions check is stuck in a loading state and never resolves to display the AdminPanel component or access denied message

Add comprehensive error handling and timeout logic to the admin route permissions check in frontend/src/App.tsx to prevent infinite loading states when the backend isCallerAdmin call hangs or fails silently

Add frontend console logging in the admin route component to output the complete state of the permissions check including: isLoading value, isError value, isOwner value, error object if present, and the exact render decision being made

Verify the useIsOwner hook in frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts is correctly processing the backend isCallerAdmin response without converting or misinterpreting the boolean value, and fix any type coercion issues that might cause a true response to be treated as false in the frontend

Verify the useIsOwner hook in frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts correctly calls the backend isCallerAdmin method and processes the boolean response without type coercion errors, ensuring true values remain true

Update the frontend admin route in App.tsx to display more detailed error information when access is denied, including the exact isOwner hook return value, whether the backend call succeeded or failed, and any error messages from the backend call

Add frontend console logging in the App.tsx admin route component to output detailed authentication flow information when the admin panel is accessed, including: 1) confirmation that the admin route component mounted, 2) the isOwner hook return value and its type, 3) the loading state, 4) the error state if present, 5) the final access decision with explanation of which condition triggered the 'Access Denied' message

Debug and resolve the error occurring in the admin panel that prevents the authenticated owner from accessing the admin dashboard, as indicated by the user's error report following Version 30 deployment
Review and fix the AdminPanel component (frontend/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx) to handle any rendering errors that may cause the component to fail or display incorrectly
Verify the admin route protection logic in frontend/src/App.tsx correctly evaluates the isCallerAdmin check and grants access to the AdminPanel when the backend returns true

Update the frontend App.tsx admin route error display to show the exact backend response from isCallerAdmin, including whether the call succeeded, the returned value, and any error details to help diagnose frontend vs backend issues
Update the useIsOwner hook in frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts to call the new password-based admin check method instead of the principal-based isPersistentAdmin method
Create a password input component in the Layout header that appears for authenticated users, allowing them to enter the admin password to access the admin panel
Update the admin route protection logic in frontend/src/App.tsx to use password-based authentication instead of principal-based checks, prompting for password if not already validated
Remove the useAdminPasswordAuth hook and all password authentication logic from the frontend, including the AdminPasswordInput component and any password validation state management

Update the Layout component in frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx to remove the AdminPasswordInput component and display the Admin navigation link directly for authenticated users without requiring password entry

Implement the useIsOwner hook in frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts to call the backend isCallerAdmin method using React Query and return the admin status based on the authenticated user's Internet Identity principal

Update the admin route protection logic in frontend/src/App.tsx to check admin access using the useIsOwner hook that validates the user's Internet Identity principal instead of password authentication, displaying the AdminPanel for authorized users
Update the Layout component to conditionally render the Admin navigation link only when the useIsOwner hook confirms the authenticated user's principal has admin access

Debug and fix the Layout component in frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx to ensure the Admin navigation button renders in the header when a user authenticates with Internet Identity, identifying any conditional logic, loading state handling, or hook dependencies that prevent the button from appearing

Verify the useIsOwner hook in frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts correctly handles authentication state changes and returns the admin status without blocking or causing rendering delays when the user logs in with Internet Identity

Add comprehensive frontend console logging in the Layout component to track the authentication lifecycle and Admin button rendering decisions, outputting: 1) when authentication state changes, 2) the isOwner hook return value and loading state, 3) whether the button render condition evaluates to true/false, 4) confirmation when the button element is added to the DOM

Ensure the Layout component header conditionally renders the Admin navigation link only after the useIsOwner hook confirms admin access, with proper fallback handling if the hook is loading or returns undefined, preventing the button from being hidden due to premature evaluation

new_feature_requests.summary: YouTube video clipping functionality
Store clip metadata including video URL, start timestamp, end timestamp, and creation date in the backend
AI-powered clip selection that identifies viral-worthy segments
YouTube login integration with automatic posting to user's channel
Implement backend algorithm to analyze video clips and score their viral potential based on factors such as engagement patterns, content type, pacing, and trending topics
Automatically detect and suggest optimal clip segments from a YouTube video that have high viral potential
Add an admin panel accessible only to the owner
Create a backend canister method to check if a caller's principal matches a hardcoded owner principal, returning a boolean
Add backend query methods to retrieve admin statistics including getTotalClipsCount and getTrendingClipsAnalytics
Implement AI-powered automatic clip generation that creates clips without manual user input

Create a backend function that automatically analyzes a YouTube video and generates multiple clip suggestions with start/end timestamps, titles, and viral scores without requiring manual timestamp selection
Add a 'Generate Clips' button in the VideoUrlForm component that triggers automatic clip generation for the entered YouTube video

Create a backend function generateClipsAutomatically that accepts a YouTube video ID and returns an array of auto-generated clip suggestions, each containing start timestamp, end timestamp, title, and viral score
Ensure the backend isCallerAdmin method correctly identifies the owner principal and returns true for the authenticated owner

Verify the backend isCallerAdmin method in backend/main.mo correctly compares the caller's principal against the owner principal and add logging to track all admin check invocations with their results
Fix admin access control - ensure authenticated owner has permission to access the admin panel
Fix the backend isCallerAdmin method in backend/main.mo to correctly authenticate the owner principal and grant admin access, ensuring it returns true when called by the authenticated owner

Verify the owner principal initialization in backend/main.mo is correctly extracting and storing the admin token from the initialization parameters, ensuring it matches the principal of the authenticated Internet Identity user

Verify the useActor hook in frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts correctly passes the admin token from URL parameters to the backend during actor initialization, ensuring the backend can associate the authenticated principal with admin privileges
